preamble: The initial devoicing of the voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] to [tʃ] is a characteristic feature of the 'Great Tone Split' in East and Southeast Asia. The historical voicing is lost in the initial consonant but remains present in the lower pitch of the resulting tone category.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Register Split, Tonogenesis, Voice Loss

examples.note: Devoicing of initial affricates led to lower-register tonal variants.
Initial voicing in affricates triggered tonal register differentiation.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
